title: Oil Jumps as Iran Resists Trump’s Offer to Help Ships Through Strait of Hormuz
summary: Oil prices fell and futures on the S&P 500 rose slightly on Sunday as investors reacted to continued uncertainty about the war in Iran.

title: China Seeks an Advantage With Both Trump and Iran as War Evolves
summary: With President Trump’s visit to Beijing looming, China is pushing Iran to negotiate even as its companies export material that could be used by Iran’s military.

title: Court Ruling Upends Abortion Access in the U.S., and Trump’s New Plan for the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Plus, the complicated legacy of Spirit Airlines.

title: The U.S. Army’s ‘Big Experiment’ in the Arctic Cold
summary: How would soldiers from places like Florida, Texas and Georgia fight and persevere in temperatures as low as minus 40 degrees?

title: Trump Backed Forced Treatment for Homeless People. Utah Shows the Challenges.
summary: State lawmakers declined to back a Trump-inspired plan to move 1,300 homeless people to a campus on the edge of Salt Lake City, but supporters are trying to keep the plan’s spirit alive.

title: Mariachi Brothers Detained by ICE Invited to Open for Kacey Musgraves After Release
summary: The teenagers, who had faced deportation after a high-profile detention, took the stage on Sunday thanks to an invitation from one of country music’s biggest stars.

title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Florida in ‘Critical Condition’
summary: A spokesman for the former mayor of New York City said Mr. Giuliani was critical but stable.

title: How the Kremlin Lures Africans Into Russia’s War in Ukraine
summary: A growing number of men across the continent say they are being promised jobs in Russia, only to be forced into the war. Some go as mercenaries, but many more are drawn unwittingly.

title: Why Are Africans Fighting in Russia’s War in Ukraine?
summary: Some see a clear explanation: Russia needs more troops, and young men in Africa desperately need jobs.

title: Drone Hits a Moscow High-Rise Days Before a Major Military Parade
summary: Air defenses in the Russian capital were breached as Ukraine expands long-range strikes.

title: The Met Gala’s Embrace of Jeff Bezos Causes a Backlash
summary: Protesters have found a perfect foil in Amazon’s founder, the gala’s lead sponsor this year.

title: The Met Makes a Statement With 9 New Mannequin Bodies
summary: The latest Costume Institute exhibition expands its ideas of who, exactly, belongs in fashion. Will the gala follow suit?

title: Met Gala 2026 Guide: Theme, Hosts, Dress Code and More
summary: What to know about this year’s celebration, including the return of a superstar last seen on the Met carpet 10 years ago.

title: School Cellphone Ban Study Finds Mixed Results
summary: Cellphone bans got devices out of students’ hands, according to the first large study. But behavior and academics have not improved, at least so far.

title: With World Distracted by Iran War, Israeli Settlers Intensify Attacks in West Bank
summary: Israel has failed to arrest a wave of violence against Palestinians, prompting military officials to urge the government to intervene.

title: Democrats Add 8 Candidates to House Battlefield Program for Midterms
summary: The party’s congressional campaign arm is promoting more contenders to take down at-risk Republicans — and is taking sides in some competitive primaries.

title: Fare on PATH Trains Increases to $3.25 as Part of Revitalization Plan
summary: Later this month, riders on the weekend will be able to travel directly from Hoboken to the World Trade Center for the first time in nearly 25 years.

title: A Medical Examiner Chases Down an Elusive Killer
summary: Unusual opioids are infiltrating street drugs. Knoxville’s top forensic doctor is on the front lines, pressing to quickly identify the most lethal.

title: What to Know About Orphines, a New Class of Deadly Opioids
summary: The drugs are 10 times more dangerous than fentanyl. They are showing up in street drugs in the South and the Midwest, and will most likely spread to other regions.

title: Chizi, Standup Comic Exiled in China, Wants to Be More Than Just ‘a Rebel Comedian’
summary: Having gotten into trouble for making jokes critical of the Chinese government, the standup comic Chizi now lives in self-imposed exile. He’s finding that freedom imposes its own constraints.

title: 3 Dead in Hantavirus Outbreak Aboard Cruise Ship, W.H.O. Says
summary: One case of hantavirus infection has been confirmed in a laboratory, the organization said on Sunday. There are five additional suspected cases.

title: What to Know About the Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on an Atlantic Cruise Ship
summary: Three passengers of the MV Hondius died after showing symptoms of the rare disease. Health authorities confirmed one case and are investigating five others.

title: What Is Hantavirus, Which Is Linked to the Deaths of 3 People Aboard a Cruise Ship?
summary: Three people aboard a cruise ship sailing the Atlantic Ocean have died from what health officials suspect were hantavirus infections.

title: The Widow of the Firefighter Slain by a Would-Be Assassin Speaks Out
summary: The conspiracy theories around the Butler, Pa., assassination attempt hurt Helen Comperatore, but in this age of distrust, partisanship and social media, she nurtures a conspiracy theory of her own.

title: Is Loving Food the Secret to Eating Less of It?
summary: The case for ditching self-denial and embracing enjoyment.

title: How Yuppies Changed America
summary: The young urban professionals of the 1980s changed our cities, our politics and our meritocratic work lives.

title: What on Earth Is Happening in California? Your Guide to a Very Weird Election.
summary: Why is the country’s most populous state stuck with such a bad choice for governor?

title: Asked and Answered
summary: We dig into reader questions.

title: After the Hong Kong Fire Killed 3 Generations of His Family, A Son Searches for Justice
summary: Lee Chun Ho lost four members of his household in last November’s apartment-tower blaze. He’s now telling an investigative committee what contractors ignored.

title: John Sterling, the Yankees’ Most Enduring Announcer, Dies at 87
summary: He called over 5,600 games, more than any other Yankees announcer, and was known for his personalized, slightly wacky home run calls.

title: The ‘Gates to Hell’ Are Dimming. That May Not Be a Good Thing.
summary: A flaming crater of gas has burned in Turkmenistan for decades. Satellite imagery suggests that the fires are decreasing, but the environmental implications are unclear.

title: A Strong El Niño May Be Coming. Global Warming Is Changing Its Effects.
summary: As the planet warms, past episodes of the natural weather phenomenon may no longer be a reliable guide of how the next one plays out.
